titleStmt.title: Letter from W. P. Henry to L. T. Silvester, 4 August, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W.P. Henry

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from WP Henry to LT Silvester, a Sergeant in the Royal Engineers. This letter is an acknowledgement of Sgt Silvester’s letter of 21 July, 1916 in which Henry advises Silveser that Sir Edward O’Farrell, Assistant Under Secretary in Dublin Castle, brought Mr. Silvester’s problem to the attention of the Police and consequently Mrs. Silvester should 'not be subjected to any further annoyance.'

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13235

correspAction.placeName: “L” Signal Company, Royal Engineers, A.P.O.S., 7. c/o G.P.O. London.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
13235 4th August 1916. Sir, Sir Edward OâFarrell desires me to inform
you that, on receipt of your letter of the 21st
ultimo, he at once called the attention of the
Police to the matters of which you complained
and as a result of their action he trusts that
your wife will not be subjected to any further
annoyance. I am,
Your obedient Servant, W. P. Henry Sergeant L. T. Silvester, 'L' Signal Company, Royal Engineers, A. P. O. S., 7. c/o G. P. O. LONDON.
